completed.title: Implement hybrid search with Qdrant + tsvector
completed.description:
Implement hybrid search combining Qdrant vector similarity with PostgreSQL tsvector full-text search and merged ranking.

Acceptance criteria:
1. Add tsvector columns + GIN indexes to memory_chunks table (new migration)
2. Create search service that runs both vector (Qdrant) and keyword (PG tsvector) searches in parallel
3. Merge results using reciprocal rank fusion (RRF)
4. Expose via GET /api/projects/{id}/memory/search endpoint (already exists, needs upgrade)
5. Worker pipeline populates tsvector on upsert
completed.outcome:
Implemented hybrid search combining Qdrant vector similarity + PostgreSQL tsvector keyword matching with Reciprocal Rank Fusion (k=60).

4. `server/src/Kontinue.Shared/Data/Migrations/KontinueDbContextModelSnapshot.cs` — Updated snapshot

completed.outcome:
Implemented 3 new MCP tools and improved 2 existing ones to solve dead-context and planning gaps:

**New tools:**
1. `kontinue_supersede_decision` — replaces an outdated decision with a new one. Archives the old decision (sets status='superseded', links superseded_by), removes its memory chunk, and creates the new decision with full context.
2. `kontinue_resolve_observation` — marks an observation/note as resolved (sets resolved_at), removes its memory chunk from search results. Cleans up stale findings.

**Improved tools:**
3. `kontinue_search_memory` — added `keyword` parameter. Now does SQL LIKE search at DB level instead of loading all chunks into memory. Falls back to full dump only when no keyword given.
4. `read_context` brief mode — now surfaces active plans with progress (done/total, next step) so agents always see the bigger picture. Full mode shows active decisions only (excludes superseded/archived).

decisions.summary: Dashboard connects via WebSocket to .NET API, builds DashboardData from StateFullEvent client-side, with camelCase JSON + string enum serialization
decisions.rationale: Direct WebSocket connection to .NET API gives real-time updates. Updating dashboard types to match .NET camelCase output is cleaner long-term than maintaining a transformation layer. Using JsonStringEnumConverter(CamelCase) means enum values like InProgress serialize as 'inProgress' which only requires changing 'in-progress' references. StateFullEvent on WS connect provides all data needed to build DashboardData client-side. Incremental events trigger re-subscribe for fresh state (simple, avoids partial state management).
decisions.alternatives: 1. Transform .NET API data to match old snake_case format in the API layer (more server work, fewer page changes) 2. Use REST polling instead of WebSocket (simpler but no real-time) 3. Build dedicated /dashboard endpoint on .NET API (adds server complexity)
decisions.context: Step 11 of Kontinue v2 plan — wiring the Electron/Vite dashboard to the .NET backend API instead of the embedded CLI web server

decisions.summary: Dedicated MigrationService project with Aspire WaitFor instead of inline migration in Api/Worker startup
decisions.rationale: Dedicated migration service project with Aspire WaitFor dependency ensures migrations run exactly once before any consumer starts. Prevents race conditions (two services trying to migrate simultaneously), keeps Api/Worker startup fast, and Aspire dashboard shows migration status clearly. Service exits after completion via lifetime.StopApplication().
decisions.alternatives: 1) Run migrations inside Api startup (EnsureCreated or MigrateAsync in Program.cs); 2) Use a shared migration extension method called by both Api and Worker; 3) Use ef-core bundle CLI tool
decisions.context: Need migrations to run before both Api and Worker to avoid race conditions and schema mismatches

decisions.summary: Use PostgreSQL stored generated column for tsvector with GIN index, merged via Reciprocal Rank Fusion (k=60)
decisions.rationale: PostgreSQL stored generated columns auto-update the tsvector when Content changes, eliminating the need for application-side logic or triggers. The GIN index on the tsvector column enables fast full-text search. Combined with Reciprocal Rank Fusion (k=60) for merging vector and keyword results, this gives robust hybrid search without additional Worker complexity.
decisions.alternatives: 1) Application-side tsvector population in Worker (like embeddings); 2) Trigger-based tsvector population; 3) Manual tsvector update in API endpoint
decisions.context: Implementing hybrid search (plan step 9) — needed to add full-text search alongside existing Qdrant vector search

decisions.summary: MCP proxy as separate file (proxy-server.ts) not shared abstraction with local server
decisions.rationale: The proxy server is a thin HTTP relay that mirrors the same 21 tool names + schemas as the local server but routes through KontinueApiClient. Keeping it as a separate file (not shared handler code) avoids coupling the local SQLite server with the proxy, making each independently testable and deployable. The proxy can diverge as the remote API evolves.
decisions.alternatives: 1) Share tool handler code between local and proxy servers using a common interface; 2) Dynamically switch the data layer at runtime via dependency injection
decisions.context: Building MCP HTTP proxy for remote .NET backend (plan step 8)

decisions.summary: Use static IEndpointRouteBuilder extension methods for minimal API endpoint organization
decisions.rationale: Keeps Program.cs clean with one MapXxx() call per domain area. Each endpoint file is self-contained with its routes and handlers. Follows .NET 10 minimal API best practices. No need for MediatR or controllers at this stage.
decisions.alternatives: MVC controllers (too heavy), MediatR (premature abstraction), Carter library (unnecessary dependency)
decisions.context: Starting REST API implementation for plan step 3

decisions.summary: Use Guid primary keys instead of int across all domain entities
decisions.rationale: Developer signal mandated this. Guids enable client-side ID generation, are safe for distributed systems (no sequence collisions), don't leak ordering, and make sync/migration between environments easier. Use Guid.CreateVersion7() for time-ordered UUIDs that maintain index locality in PostgreSQL.
decisions.alternatives: int/auto-increment PKs — rejected per developer signal. Int PKs leak ordering information and require DB roundtrip for ID generation.

decisions.summary: Use Activities (System.Diagnostics) and custom Meters for observability — build all traces with explicit ActivitySource and Meter instrumentation
decisions.rationale: Developer signal mandated this. Activities provide distributed tracing context propagation. Custom Meters expose domain-specific metrics (chunks embedded, signals processed, tasks completed). Combined with Aspire's OpenTelemetry integration, this gives full observability without vendor lock-in.
decisions.alternatives: Rely only on Aspire's auto-instrumentation — rejected because it only covers HTTP/gRPC boundaries, not domain-specific operations like embedding pipeline, signal processing, or task state transitions.

decisions.summary: Use LoggerMessage source-generated extension methods on ILogger instead of default logger methods
decisions.rationale: Developer signal mandated this. LoggerMessage source gen avoids boxing, string interpolation allocation, and log-level checks at call sites. The [LoggerMessage] attribute generates high-performance logging methods at compile time — zero allocation for disabled log levels.
decisions.alternatives: Default ILogger.LogInformation/LogError — rejected per developer signal. Allocates on every call even when log level is disabled.

decisions.summary: JsonPolymorphism with [JsonDerivedType] and $type discriminator for WebSocket wire protocol
decisions.rationale: All WebSocket messages (server→client events and client→server commands) share a single base type WsMessage with $type discriminator. This gives: (1) clean pattern matching on both server (C# switch expression) and client (TypeScript dispatch map), (2) type-safe (de)serialization with no manual type checking, (3) extensible — adding a new event type = add one class + one [JsonDerivedType] attribute. Combined with STJ source generators for zero-reflection AOT-safe serialization.
decisions.alternatives: Separate endpoint per event type — rejected because WebSocket is a single channel. Manual discriminator parsing — rejected because [JsonPolymorphic] handles it natively with compile-time safety. MessagePack binary protocol — rejected because JSON is debuggable in browser DevTools and the payload sizes don't warrant binary.

decisions.summary: Granular patch events over WebSocket instead of full-state blobs on every change
decisions.rationale: Current SSE sends the entire DashboardData object on every event. With multiple projects and workspaces, this becomes expensive. New approach: send state.full only on connect/reconnect, then send granular events (task.updated, signal.received, etc.) that the client patches into local state. Reduces bandwidth, enables fine-grained UI updates (animate only the changed card), and scales to many concurrent projects.
decisions.alternatives: Keep full-state approach — rejected because it doesn't scale to multi-project dashboard with frequent updates across projects. Server-side diff computation — rejected as unnecessarily complex; granular events are simpler since the server already knows what changed.

decisions.summary: Bounded Channel<T> for per-connection backpressure with DropOldest policy on outbound queues
decisions.rationale: If a client's network is slow, unbounded outbound queues would grow unbounded in server memory. BoundedChannelOptions with DropOldest ensures: (1) slow clients don't cause server OOM, (2) clients that reconnect get state.full anyway so dropped events are recoverable, (3) fast clients never block the broadcast loop. Inbound channels are also bounded to prevent malicious clients from flooding the dispatcher.
decisions.alternatives: Unbounded channels — rejected due to OOM risk with slow clients. TCP backpressure only (no channel) — rejected because it would block the broadcast fan-out loop for all connections when one is slow.

decisions.summary: Incremental 4-phase migration: backend alongside CLI → MCP proxy → multi-workspace dashboard → deprecate embedded server
decisions.rationale: Big-bang migration would break existing workflows. Incremental approach: Phase 1 deploys the new backend while CLI still works standalone (kontinue sync exports data). Phase 2 adds MCP proxy mode so agents can use either local or remote. Phase 3 ships the multi-workspace dashboard. Phase 4 deprecates the embedded web server. Each phase is independently deployable and reversible.
decisions.alternatives: Big-bang rewrite — rejected because it would leave agents without a working backend during the transition. Keep CLI as primary and add API as optional — rejected because the goal is to make the centralized backend the source of truth.

decisions.summary: PostgreSQL as relational store with multi-tenant schema: workspace → project → entities
decisions.rationale: PostgreSQL gives us: (1) tsvector for hybrid full-text search alongside Qdrant vectors, (2) native JSON columns for flexible metadata, (3) row-level security if we need tenant isolation later, (4) proven scalability for the entity model (tasks, decisions, signals, sessions, plans, memory chunks). Multi-tenancy via workspace_id FK on projects rather than separate databases — simpler queries, single connection pool.
decisions.alternatives: Keep SQLite per-project — rejected because it can't serve a centralized multi-project dashboard or support concurrent agent writes. Separate database per workspace — rejected as premature; FK-based tenancy is simpler and sufficient at this scale.

decisions.summary: Qdrant for vector/semantic search replacing keyword-based search_memory
decisions.rationale: Current search_memory uses SQL LIKE / FTS5 keyword matching which misses semantic relationships (e.g. searching "auth" won't find a chunk about "login tokens"). Qdrant provides: (1) cosine similarity on embeddings, (2) payload filtering (by project_id, type, date), (3) hybrid search (combine vector score with keyword match). Metadata stays in PostgreSQL; Qdrant stores only vector + minimal payload for filtering. Linked by chunk_id.
decisions.alternatives: pgvector extension in PostgreSQL — rejected because it adds embedding concerns to the relational DB and Qdrant has better filtering/indexing for pure vector workloads. Pinecone/Weaviate — rejected because Qdrant is self-hosted, fits Aspire orchestration, and has a .NET client.

decisions.summary: MCP server becomes a thin HTTP proxy (stdio → API) — agents still launch it as a local process
decisions.rationale: AI agents expect MCP servers as local stdio processes. Changing that requires MCP client changes in Claude/Copilot/Cursor which we don't control. A thin proxy preserves the agent contract: agent launches `kontinue mcp`, speaks stdio JSON-RPC, proxy translates to HTTP calls against Kontinue.Api. Zero agent-side changes. Remote MCP over HTTP can be added later as a second transport when MCP clients mature.
decisions.alternatives: Remote MCP over HTTP directly — rejected because MCP HTTP transport is not yet widely supported by agents. Embed the full backend in the CLI — rejected because that's the current architecture we're moving away from.

decisions.summary: Aspire AppHost orchestrates 4 resources: Kontinue.Api (ASP.NET Core), Kontinue.Worker (background service), PostgreSQL, and Qdrant
decisions.rationale: Aspire provides service discovery, health checks, resource lifecycle, and local dev orchestration out of the box. Splitting Api and Worker separates the request-handling hot path from background jobs (embedding pipeline, decay, digests, stale detection) so neither starves the other.
decisions.alternatives: Single monolith API with hosted services — rejected because background embedding and decay work would compete with request threads. Separate microservices without Aspire — rejected because orchestration and service discovery would need manual Docker Compose wiring.

decisions.summary: Use raw WebSockets with Channels, ArrayPool, and STJ source-gen instead of SignalR for real-time events
decisions.rationale: SignalR adds abstraction overhead (hub protocol negotiation, MessagePack/JSON framing, automatic reconnect logic) that isn't needed when we control both client and server. Raw WebSockets with .NET Channels give us: (1) backpressure control via bounded channels, (2) zero-allocation reads/writes via ArrayPool<byte>.Shared and MemoryPool, (3) full control over the wire protocol, (4) AOT-compatible serialization with STJ source generators, (5) polymorphic event discrimination with [JsonDerivedType] attributes. The dashboard is a known client — we don't need SignalR's broad transport fallback (SSE, long polling).
decisions.alternatives: SignalR — rejected because it adds unnecessary abstraction, doesn't give fine-grained control over buffer management, and its hub protocol adds framing overhead. SSE (current) — rejected because it's one-way only and doesn't support binary or bidirectional messaging.

decisions.summary: Use navy/beige branding with dark-gray-blue dark theme instead of pure black
decisions.rationale: Developer explicitly requested dark-gray-blue dark theme and navy/beige brand identity. Using slate-navy (hsl 222 47% 11%) for dark bg and navy (hsl 222 47% 30%) for primary with beige (hsl 38 40% 92%) for accents.
decisions.alternatives: 1) Pure black (#09090b) for dark mode (default shadcn). 2) Slate dark blue. 3) Custom navy/beige brand palette.
decisions.context: Developer signal: don't use black for dark theme, use dark-gray-blue. Also use navy + beige as branding colors.

decisions.summary: Fix stale task detection: normalize JS cutoff timestamp to SQLite datetime format
decisions.rationale: SQLite datetime('now') produces 'YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS' (space separator) while JS toISOString() produces 'YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS.sssZ' (T separator). Since space (ASCII 32) < T (ASCII 84), string comparison 'updated_at < cutoff' was always true. Fix normalizes JS cutoff to SQLite's space-separated format.
decisions.alternatives: 1) Use SQLite datetime functions in the query instead of JS. 2) Store all timestamps in ISO format.
decisions.context: In-progress tasks always showed as stale on the dashboard regardless of when they were started

decisions.summary: Use color-mix() for all theme-aware color tinting instead of hardcoded hex per theme
decisions.rationale: color-mix() lets us compute badge/tag/signal backgrounds from the semantic color variables (--green, --red, etc.) mixed with --bg2, making colors automatically correct in both light and dark themes. This eliminates the need for [data-theme] overrides.
decisions.alternatives: Hardcoded hex values per theme (old approach, more verbose), CSS custom properties per semantic color pair (too many variables)
decisions.context: Redesigning dashboard CSS from GitHub-style to shadcn/ui design system

decisions.summary: GitHub/Linear integration: link-only approach first, full API sync deferred
decisions.rationale: Started with link-only approach (no API calls to GitHub/Linear) to keep it simple and avoid token/auth complexity. A single link_external tool handles both providers. Full API sync (create issues, close on done) can be layered on top later with a github_token config field. This minimum viable approach ships the database schema and linking infrastructure needed for any future sync.
decisions.alternatives: Full GitHub API sync with create/close issues; Linear-only integration; Separate tools per provider

decisions.summary: Plan creation is mandatory whenever a goal has 3+ steps or multiple phases — agent must not wait to be asked
decisions.rationale: Without a mandatory trigger, agents default to starting tasks immediately for multi-step work, losing the roadmap and context recovery benefits of plans. Making it a hard rule (3+ steps = plan first) eliminates the ambiguity.
decisions.alternatives: Keep it advisory but add stronger emphasis (rejected — agents interpret suggestions loosely under pressure)
decisions.context: Plans were only being created when the user explicitly mentioned them. Agent should detect the need from the goal itself and create plans proactively.

decisions.summary: Add lifecycle management to decisions and observations, keyword search to memory, and surface plans in brief context
decisions.rationale: The core problem is that Kontinue entities are write-only — they have creation but no lifecycle. Decisions and observations accumulate forever, polluting context. The fix is to add targeted lifecycle tools: supersede for decisions (links old→new), resolve for observations (marks as addressed), keyword search for memory (so agents don't load everything), and surface plans in brief mode so agents use them. These require minimal schema changes (one new column each) while solving the biggest pain points.
decisions.alternatives: 1. Add lifecycle to every entity type (decisions, notes, chunks) — rejected as too complex for one pass; focus on the highest-impact changes first. 2. Build a separate garbage collector tool — rejected because it's a band-aid; the real fix is giving entities lifecycle states. 3. Focus on three targeted changes: (a) add supersede_decision tool, (b) add keyword search to search_memory, (c) surface plans in brief mode, (d) add resolve_observation tool — chosen because these directly address the dead-context and planning gaps with minimal schema change.
decisions.context: User asked to audit Kontinue tools for gaps preventing autonomous agent behavior. Key complaints: agents read dead/stale context, plans are never used, no way to clean up obsolete decisions/observations.

decisions.summary: Rewrite agent instructions from tool-usage guides into an autonomous-agent operating model with intent persistence, proactive execution, and compaction recovery
decisions.rationale: Current instructions (CLAUDE.md, copilot-instructions.md, cursor rules, GitHub prompts/agents) are identical copies of a tool-usage reference. They tell the agent WHAT tools exist and HOW to call them, but never WHY or WHEN in the context of autonomous work. Missing entirely: (1) Identifying and persisting user intent as the driver of all work. (2) Translating intent into goals and executing them without waiting for step-by-step user direction. (3) Using Kontinue as source of truth instead of the chat. (4) Recovering context after conversation compaction. (5) The agent's role as an autonomous worker, not a report generator. The user explicitly stated: 'the idea of Kontinue is not only persistent memory but more autonomy and proactiveness' and 'return the chat to its main functionality — a communication gate not a source of truth.'
decisions.alternatives: 1. Patch existing instructions with a few new sections — rejected because the structure itself is wrong; a tool reference doesn't become an operating model by adding sections. 2. Create a separate philosophy doc and link to it — rejected because agents read CLAUDE.md/copilot-instructions.md as their primary instructions; splitting the operating model into a separate file reduces compliance. 3. Full rewrite with new structure: Operating Model first, then Tool Reference as appendix — chosen because it puts the mindset and workflow front and center.
decisions.context: User feedback: 'you correctly used the kontinue update task tool proactively, however, what is next you just display to the user your observations, show recommendations... The idea of Kontinue is not only persistent memory but more autonomy and proactiveness. You receive a task from user you must identify the user intent, translate that into goal and work to achieve user's goal proactively and autonomously... It is required practice to remember (persist) user intent(s), how it translated into tasks (goals), the observations, the progress... return the chat to its main functionality, a communication gate not a source of truth. Important, we need to guide/instruct the ai agent how to compact the conversation given kontinue and how to use it after compacting.'

Observation: Tool audit findings — critical gaps preventing autonomous workflow:

**DEAD CONTEXT PROBLEM:**
1. Decisions have no lifecycle — cannot be superseded, archived, or marked obsolete. Old decisions accumulate forever and pollute read_context/search_memory.
2. Observations (notes) have no lifecycle — no way to resolve/archive them. They accumulate as memory chunks indefinitely.
3. search_memory loads ALL chunks into memory then filters — no keyword search at DB level. Returns everything regardless of relevance.
4. read_context brief mode shows ALL in-progress tasks and recent decisions with no relevance filtering. On a mature project this is token-expensive.
5. memory_chunks content is never updated — when a task is completed, the chunk still says "Task: ..." with original description, not the outcome.

**PLANNING GAPS:**
6. Plans exist but are disconnected from the autonomous workflow. No instruction tells agents WHEN to use plans vs tasks. Plans don't surface in brief mode.
7. Plans have no link to tasks — a plan's steps can't reference tasks, and vice versa.
8. Active plans should surface in brief context (currently only in full mode).

**MISSING TOOLS:**
9. No `kontinue_supersede_decision` — when a decision is replaced by a newer one, the old one should be archived/linked to show the evolution.
10. No `kontinue_archive_observation` or `kontinue_resolve_note` — observations pile up with no way to mark them as addressed.
11. No `kontinue_update_observation` — cannot update an observation's content after creation.
12. search_memory has no keyword parameter — it's a dump tool, not a search tool. Need actual keyword filtering at the DB level.
13. No way for an agent to clean up stale memory — no prune/gc/archive mechanism.

**INSTRUCTION GAPS:**
14. Agent instructions don't mention when to use plans vs tasks. Plans should be used for multi-step work that spans across tasks.
15. Brief mode should show active plans with progress so agents know the bigger picture.
Task: Audit and improve Kontinue MCP tools
Files: cli/src/mcp/server.ts,cli/src/store/queries.ts

Observation: User's core philosophy for Kontinue: (1) Agents must be AUTONOMOUS and PROACTIVE — when given a task like "audit the project", don't just report findings, translate into goals and work to fix them. (2) Chat is a COMMUNICATION GATE, not a source of truth — all observations, decisions, progress must be persisted in Kontinue tools, not just displayed in conversation. (3) User INTENT must be persisted — capture what the user asked for, how it was translated into tasks, and the progress toward those goals. (4) Conversation COMPACTION recovery — agents must know how to use Kontinue to recover full context after the conversation is compressed. This is the foundational design principle for all agent instructions.
Task: Redesign agent instructions

plans.title: Kontinue v2 — .NET Backend Platform
plans.goal: Migrate Kontinue from embedded Node.js/SQLite CLI to a centralized .NET 10 platform with PostgreSQL, Qdrant, raw WebSockets, and multi-workspace dashboard, orchestrated by .NET Aspire.

steps.content: Scaffold Aspire solution structure (AppHost + Api + Worker + Shared projects with PG and Qdrant resources)
Define domain model and EF Core entities — port SQLite schema to EF Core with multi-tenant workspace→project hierarchy and initial migration
Implement core REST API — CRUD for tasks/decisions/observations/signals/plans/memory_chunks/checkpoints/handoffs matching MCP tool surface
Implement Worker embedding pipeline — background service for Qdrant vector upserts + decay job + digest compression
Build WebSocket infrastructure — ConnectionManager + ClientConnection with ArrayPool read/write loops + bounded Channels with DropOldest
Define wire protocol — WsMessage [JsonPolymorphic] + [JsonDerivedType] for all events/commands + KontinueJsonContext source-gen serializer
Integrate WebSocket with API — endpoints publish granular events to broadcast Channel + fan-out to subscribed connections + state.full on connect
Build MCP HTTP proxy — new CLI mode (kontinue mcp --backend=remote) translating stdio JSON-RPC to API HTTP calls
Implement hybrid search — Qdrant vector similarity + PostgreSQL tsvector keyword matching with merged ranking
Data sync/migration tooling — kontinue sync command to export local SQLite to the API with conflict resolution
Dashboard workspace routing — URL structure /:workspaceSlug/:projectId/ + workspace switcher + workspace overview page

Auth layer — API key per project for MCP proxy + session-based auth for dashboard + workspace membership enforcement
Deprecate embedded web server — kontinue web redirects to dashboard URL + retain offline SQLite fallback + migration guide
